Notice And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-25-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom):
Daniel O. Nickfardjam, Esq., QUILL & ARROW, LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiff.

SCOTT D. SHARP, Esq., LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, (213) 250-1800, for Defendant.

RE: Defendant NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay.

Ruling - ARGUED. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied. The court rejects nonsignatory defendant's argument that it can compel arbitration based on equitable estoppel. The equitable estoppel rule holds that a party invoking one part of a contract cannot object to the other side invoking some other part. In the arbitration context, the "application of equitable estoppel as the basis for allowing a nonsignatory to enforce an arbitration clause is that the claims the plaintiff asserts against the nonsignatory must be dependent upon, or founded in and inextricably intertwined with, the underlying contractual obligations of the agreement containing the arbitration clause." (Goldman v. KPMG, LLP (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 209, 217-218.) Equitable estoppel precludes a party from claiming the benefits of a contract while simultaneously attempting to avoid the burdens that contract imposes. (Philadelphia Indemnity Ins. Co. v. SMG Holdings, Inc. (2019) 44 Cal.App.5th 834, 841.) The central issue is whether the plaintiffs' claims are "intimately founded in and intertwined' with the underlying contract obligations." (Martha Ochoa v. Ford Motor Company (Ford Motor Warranty Cases) (2023) 89 Cal.App.5th 1324, __, 306 Cal.Rptr.3d 611, 618, quoting (and then disagreeing with) Felisilda v. FCA US LLC (2020) 53 Cal.App.5th 486.) At first blush the claims seem intertwined: After all, plaintiff needed to enter into the Retail Installment Contract - Simple Finance Charge (With Arbitration Provision) ("RISC") to purchase the vehicle and obtain the warranties. Nevertheless, closer review demonstrates that plaintiff's action is not in fact intertwined with the RISC. Plaintiff is suing for violations of the Song-Beverly Act and fraud that is unrelated to the RISC. As Ochoa explains, the cited warranties are independent from the sale agreement. (See also Montemayor v. Ford Motor Company (Issued June 26, 2023) WL 4181909 [nonsignatory car manufacturer could not enforce arbitration clause based on equitable estoppel].) The court notes Felisdilda is distinguishable. There, the plaintiff asserted a single cause of action against the manufacturer and dealership and the dealership sought arbitration. In "Felisilda, it was the dealership-a signatory to the purchase agreement-that moved to compel arbitration rather than the non-signatory manufacturer." (Ngo v. BMW of North America, LLC (9th Cir. 2022) 23 F.4th 942, 950; see also e.g., Glassburg v. Ford Motor Company (C.D. Cal., Nov. 2, 2021, No. 221CV01333ODWMAAX) 2021 WL 5086358, at *3; In re ZF-TRW Airbag Control Units Products Liability Litigation (C.D. Cal., Sept. 27, 2021, No. LAML1902905JAKFFMX) 2021 WL 5069003, at *5 & n.1.) In this case, plaintiff is only suing the manufacturer for statutory violations and fraud unrelated to the RISC. "Plaintiff is seeking to recover for Defendant's alleged breaches of its manufacturer warranties, which arose independently of the Lease Agreement." (Tawil v. BMW of North America, LLC (C.D. Cal., May 12, 2022, No. CV2109257RSWLJPRX) 2022 WL 19076675, at *3.) In other words, the fact that plaintiffs invoke warranties does not mean they invoke the RISC.

Counsel is to submit a proposed order which tracks verbatim the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)

Notice And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-12-2023 Continued To Jul-25-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Camran Pakbaz, Esq., Quil & Arrow, LLP, 310-933-4271, for Plaintiff.
Scott Sharp, Esq., Lewis Brisbois Bisgard & Smith, LLP, 213-250-1800, Scott.Sharp@lewisbrisbois.com, for Defendant Nissan North America, Inc.

Plaintiff contested the court's tentative ruling late. Plaintiff's contest email is received by the court at 6:20 p.m.

Discussion is held with plaintiff's counsel as to why a timely opposition was not filed. Plaintiff's opposition to the motion was filed on july 11, 2023, 13 days late. Plaintiff states that their earlier opposition, (timely filed) was rejected by Rm. 103 for improperly noticed hearing date and time and that plaintiff was not aware of the rejection unitl July 11, 2023.

The Court a modifies it's tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action continued to July 25, 2023 at 9:30 a.m. Dept. 302.
